scope_boundary: SCIF sanitized commit replay probe only. The probe records two valid local synthetic routes and six expected-invalid controls for secret canary leakage, live handle leakage, missing zeroization, overbroad context admission, unapproved destination or inactive approval, and missing residual ownership. It uses public-safe synthetic records and does not test a deployed kernel, sandbox, vault, approval service, side-channel boundary, runtime prompt-injection containment system, or real secret-handle workflow.
transition_reason: The SCIF sanitized commit replay probe is useful because it makes a public-safe sanitized-output path and six leak/authority/lifecycle negative controls executable as a local book-gate replay. It remains a synthetic local probe, not deployed security evidence, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for deployed-kernel behavior, sandbox isolation, side-channel safety, prompt-injection containment, secret-handle safety, approval-service behavior, least-privilege context behavior, privacy, security, or the Security Kernel chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable security-kernel trace
real or externally reviewable sandbox-isolation evidence
secret-handle or vault workflow evidence with public-safe redaction
least-privilege context-admission trace over real or externally reviewable workflows
residual leak-risk review with independent review
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: secret canary in candidate output is blocked before commit
live handle in candidate output is blocked before commit
missing zeroization lifecycle step is blocked before commit
overbroad context admission is blocked before commit
unapproved destination or inactive approval is blocked before commit
missing residual ownership is blocked before commit
valid sanitized commit routes record no deployed vault, sandbox, network, private-source, job-dispatch, or publication effects
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: SCIF sanitized commit probe validator failure
secret canary or live handle appears in the public result record
local temp paths or private source material appear in the public result record
invalid leak, missing-zeroize, overbroad-context, unapproved-destination, or missing-residual control accepted as valid
probe described as deployed kernel, sandbox-isolation, side-channel, prompt-injection-containment, secret-handle, approval-service, privacy, security, or support-state evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed SCIF or security-kernel claim after deployed or externally reviewable kernel traces, sandbox-isolation evidence, side-channel review, runtime prompt-injection containment evidence, secret-handle or vault workflow evidence, approval-service behavior, least-privilege context-admission traces, residual leak-risk review, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
limitations: no real secret-handle or vault workflow
does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent security, sandbox, side-channel, prompt-injection, vault, approval-service, privacy, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: no deployed or externally reviewable security-kernel trace
no sandbox-isolation or side-channel review evidence
no real secret-handle or vault workflow trace
non_claims: does not promote SCIF sanitized commit replay evidence above argument

does not prove deployed-kernel behavior, sandbox isolation, side-channel safety, prompt-injection containment, secret-handle safety, approval-service behavior, least-privilege context behavior, privacy, security, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Security Kernel and Digital SCIFs chapter core claim
